rag_rules: AGREE contracts for component ports belong in component type scopes; implementation-level bindings go in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope; do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should be placed in appropriate AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Component type contracts should relate visible inputs to visible outputs without referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
If AGREE references unresolved identifiers, do not invent declarations; use only visible identifiers from the current scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements within annex blocks.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Do not use AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing properties not exposed as typed ports or constants
Unresolved identifier diagnostics and repair patterns for model dependencies
AGREE validator diagnostics and minimal repair patterns
